justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified with table showing 99.9% Onfido makes 99.95% target impossible, combined best-case calculated at 99.85%. (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended: async KYC with pending verification state, circuit breaker, fallback provider, SLA revision. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'single biggest operational risk'. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling issue identified with specific recommendation to split liveness and readiness probes. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to Coach K / MJ for architecture decision on SLA.

observations.text: SLA math is not just identified but quantified in a clear table with monthly downtime figures — goes beyond the rubric requirement
Circuit breaker gap is thoroughly analyzed with specific parameters (50% error rate, 30s window, 60s open)
Health check issue includes actionable fix: split /health/live and /health/ready
Mitigation options are extensive: async KYC, circuit breaker, retry with backoff, secondary KYC provider, request queuing
Thread pool exhaustion scenario is specifically modeled (100 connections, 20 req/s, 5s timeout = exhausted in 5 seconds)
Thundering herd after recovery identified — a subtle failure mode not in the rubric
Recommendations are prioritized into must-haves, should-haves, and nice-to-haves with clear rationale
Escalations properly routed to other team members for architecture, security, and implementation concerns
